deaccent.description: Convert string to replace accented characters.

dejunk.description: Convert string to replace all but simple ASCII chars. New in 2.6.0.3.

flt.description: Convert string or int to float, if possible.

levenshtein.description: Levenshtein edit-distance between two strings. New in 2.0.2.9

num.description: Convert string to number, if possible. (Also accepts num arg).

split.description: Split a string using the given char as separator.

stem.description: Compute stem of noun and verb. Return lowercase string.

strchar.description: Index to numth char of string

strchr.description: Find first occurrence of char in string. Return string headed by char.

strchrcount.description: Count the occurrences of a character in a string.

strclean.description: Remove leading, trailing, and repeat whitespace separators.

strcontains.description: Check if str1is contained in str2.

strcontainsnocase.description: Check if str1 is contained in str2, ignoring letter case.

strendswith.description: Does given str end with given suffix_str

strstartswith.description: Does given str starts with given suffix_str

strequal.description: Check if str1 is identical to str2.

strisalpha.description: Check if str is all alphabetic chars.

strisdigit.description: Check if str is all numeric chars.

strislower.description: Check if first char of string is lowercase.

strisupper.description: Check if first char of string is uppercase.

strnotequal.description: Check if str1 differs from str2.

strequalnocase.description: Check if str1 is identical to str2, ignoring letter case.

strnotequalnocase.description: Check if str1 differs from str2, ignoring letter case.

strlessthan.description: Check if str1 is lexically before str2.

strgreaterthan.description: Check if str1 is lexically after str2.

strpiece.description: Fetch substring of string from start_num to end_num indexes. Zero-based

strrchr.description: Find last occurrence of char in string. Return string headed by char

strsubst.description: Replace substring old_str of a string str with new_str.

strtrim.description: Remove leading and trailing whitespace from str.

struniquechars.description: Returns all the unique characters in a string

str.description: Convert numeric value to string. (Also accepts string arg).

suffix.description: True if suffixStr may be the suffix of wordStr.

unpackdirs.description: Unpack directory names from a full directory path string.

xmlstr.description: Convert chars to appropriate chars for an XML/HTML file.

batchstart.description: Check if start of a batch analysis. True if current file is first in a batch run.

debug.description: No op. Convenient breakpoint for debugging from C++ compiler.

exitpass.description: Exit from the current pass file immediately, without performing rule matching (if any).

exittopopup.description: Exit from current pass, invoking a popup if within VisualText.

fail.description: In CHECK Region, abort the rule that has just matched. Noop elsewhere. See succeed().

findana.description: See if named project is already resident in memory. (New in 1.6)

getpopupdata.description: Get the data typed in by a call to exittopopup().

hitconf.description: Calculate the keyword density. Fudge factor is typically 3 to 20.

interactive.description: True if analyzer is being run in an interactive environment, e.g., inside VisualText.

permuten.description: Permute integers 0 to (tot_num - 1) such that array element k will never be equal to k.

succeed.description: In CHECK Region and CODE Region, succeed without executing further code. See fail().

system.description: Execute str as operating system command.

today.description: Format the current date and time as a string.

cbuf.description: Output to buffer that user supplied for the current parse (standalone or embedded only).

cout.description: Output to standard output or user-supplied stream (the latter in standalone or embedded only).

coutreset.description: Bind cout() stream to the given filename.

inputrangetofile.description: Print a range of chars from the input text to an output stream. (Listed with Parse Tree Functions)

LJ.description: Left-justify num in given field size.

percentstr.description: Format a percentage (right-justified in field of 3 chars).

rightjustifynum.description: Right-justify num in given field size.

spellcandidates.description: Make a list of space separated candidates for spell-correcting given word.

spellcorrect.description: Correct spelling of word. If word is not known, returns best spelling guess.

spellword.description: Check if word is a known English word.

strspellcandidate.description: Select best spell-correct candidate for a word, given a list of space-separated candidates (as returned by spellcandidates, e.g.)

strspellcompare.description: Measure spelling "distance" between two given words. The smaller the number, the fewer the corrections to get from one to the other.

arraylength.description: Count number of values in given variable or expr. (Like a call-by-reference)

eltnode.description: Fetch the first node that matched the nth element of a rule.

group.description: Perform a reduction in the @POST region. Replaces the old group action. (2.3.1.9 return the created node.)

inputrange.description: Return a substring of the input text as specified by start and end offsets.

inputrange1.description: Print a range of chars from the input text to an output stream.

lasteltnode.description: Fetch the last node that matched the nth element of a rule.

phrasetext.description: Fetch the text that matched the right hand side phrase of a rule. Analogous to $text.

phraseraw.description: Fetch the raw text that matched the right hand side phrase of a rule. Analogous to $raw

pncopyvars.description: Copy a node's variables to the suggested node of a rule match. Must be called from the POST Region and can be considered a new-style NLP++ action.

pncopyvars1.description: Copy a node's variables to the suggested node of a rule match. Must be called from the POST Region and can be considered a new-style NLP++ action.

pncopyvars2.description: Copy a node's variables to the suggested node of a rule match. Must be called from the POST Region and can be considered a new-style NLP++ action.

pninsert.description: Insert nonliteral node with name into parse tree. If after_n == 1, insert after pnode, else if 0 then before pnode.

pnmakevar.description: Make a variable for a given pnode.

pnname.description: Fetch the name of a pnode.

pnnext.description: Fetch a pnode's right sibling, if any.

pnprev.description: Fetch a pnode's left sibling, if any.

pnrename.description: Rename given pnode. Returns the interned name.

pnreplaceval.description: Replace the value of pnode's variable.

pnroot.description: Fetch the root of a parse tree.

pndown.description: Fetch the child of a parse tree node.

pnsingletdown.description: Fetch the child of a parse tree node, obeying rules about going down a "singlet chain." I.e., get child only if no branching and if not going past node with BASE flag set.

pnup.description: Fetch a pnode's parent, if any. Only leftmost pnode in a list has a parent.

pnvarnames.description: Fetch a node's variable names as an array.

varinlist.description: Find var_str variable's value(s) in any pnode that matched elt_numth element

kbdumptree.description: Create a dump file of knowledge base concept.

writekb.description: Writes the entire KB out to a directory under the kb directory

take.description: Execute commands in a knowledge base command file (.KB file)

findroot.description: Find the root concept of the knowledge base (named concept).

findconcept.description: Find and return the concept with name str under the parent concept con.

findconcept1.description: Find concept number num under the parent concept con.

findattr.description: Fetch attribute named str belonging to concept con.

findvals.description: Fetch list of values for a concept's attribute.

numval.description: Fetch numeric-value of attribute (must be first).

fltval.description: Fetch float value of attribute (must be first).

strval.description: Fetch string-value of attribute (must be first).

conval.description: Fetch concept-value of attribute (must be first).

attrwithval.description: Check if concept's attribute has given value (multiple-value aware). Note that second two arguments must be STR.

inheritval.description: Find string attribute's value up the hierarchy. con is the current concept, topCon is the root to be searched up to, and name is the name of the attribute. If hier==0, goes to root of KB.

conceptpath.description: Fetch entire path of given concept as a string.

pathconcept.description: Fetch concept, given the path str.

wordpath.description: Get entire path of dictionary concept for the given string.

findwordpath.description: Find entire path of dictionary concept for the given string. (If not present, don't add the word.)

wordindex.description: Fetch index entry for dictionary concept. Gets index concept that str would be added under.

findhierconcept.description: Find a concept in the subhierarchy of a given concept. e.g.: G("returnedConcept") = findhierconcept("child",G("ancestor")); finds the concept named "child" anywhere under the concept pointed to by G("ancestor").

dictfindword.description: Find dictionary concept in dictionary hierarchy of KB.

dictfirst.description: Fetch the first word-concept in the KB dictionary hierarchy. (New in 1.6)

dictnext.description: Fetch the word following the given word in the KB dictionary hierarchy. (New in 1.6)

attrexists.description: Find attribute and value pair in the subhierarchy of a concept.

attrchange.description: Replace all matching attribute-value pairs in the given hierarchy to have the new string value, new_s.

down.description: Fetch the first child of given concept.

up.description: Fetch the parent of the given concept.

prev.description: Fetch the left or previous sibling of concept.

next.description: Fetch the right or next sibling of concept.

nextattr.description: Fetch the next attribute in a list of attributes

nextval.description: Fetch the next value in a list of values.

getsval.description: Fetch value as a string from a numeric or string-valued KB value object.

getstrval.description: Fetch the string value from a KB value object.

makeconcept.description: Make concept under given parent concept. Optionally as the numth child of the parent. num==0 or absent places concept at end of list of children. name is the name of new concept.

addattr.description: Add an attribute with no value to given concept.

addsval.description: Add numeric value num as a string to concept con's attribute called name.

addstrval.description: Add str as string value to concept con's attribute called name.

addnumval.description: Add num as numeric value to concept con's attribute called name.

addconval.description: Add concept value con_val to concept's name attribute.

getconcept.description: Get or make named concept under parent.

addword.description: Add dictionary concept to the dictionary within the KB, if not present. In either case, fetch the dictionary concept for the word.

dictgetword.description: Same as addword; more principled function name.

sortconsbyattr.description: Given an array (multi-valued variable) of KB concepts, sort the concepts by the given attribute. numeric_b = 1 if num, 0 if string type attribute. descending_b = 1 if descending order, 0 if ascending. E.g., G("sorted") = sortconsbyattr(G("cons"),"count",1,0); will sort an array of KB concepts by their "count" attribute in ascending order, assigning a new sorted array to G("sorted"). The given array in G("cons") is left unaltered. New in 1.6

rmconcept.description: Remove concept con from Knowledge Base. Removes entire subhierarchy.

rmchild.description: Remove child named str from parent concept con.

rmchild1.description: Remove number num child of parent concept con.

rmvals.description: Remove attributes and values of concept con's attribute named str.

rmval.description: Remove value val from attribute attr.

rmattrval.description: Remove value named str2 from attribute named str1 under concept con. Also removes the attribute.

rmattrs.description: Remove concept's attributes (except for system internal ones).

rmattr.description: Remove concept's named attr and values.

prunephrases.description: Remove all phrases from given subhierarchy.

replaceval.description: Replace named attribute's value(s) with str.

replaceval1.description: Replace named attribute's value(s) with num.

replaceval2.description: Replace named attribute's value(s) with concept con_val

renamechild.description: Rename con's numth child concept to name.

renameattr.description: Rename con's named attribute to new.

movecleft.description: Move concept before previous sibling. (Moves concept to the left in its list.)

movecright.description: Move concept after next sibling. (Moves concept to the right in its list.)

phraselength.description: Get number of nodes in concept's phrase.

nodeconcept.description: Fetch the concept that node is a proxy for. (Changed in 1.6)

nodeowner.description: Fetch the concept that owns the node's phrase. (NEW in 1.6)

findnode.description: Find first named node in phrase.

findnode1.description: Find the numth node in phrase.

listnode.description: Fetch the first node in given node's list.

firstnode.description: Fetch the first node in phrase.

lastnode.description: Fetch the last node in phrase.

makephrase.description: Make a phrase in con by creating named node.

addcnode.description: Add named node at end of con's phrase.

addnode.description: Add named node as numth in phrase.

renamenode.description: Rename phrase's named node to new.

renamenode1.description: Rename phrase's numth node to new.

abs.description: Compute the absolute value of an integer.

dballocstmt.description: Allocate statement handle for the currently open database.

dbbindcol.description: Bind a database table's column to an NLP++ variable.

dbexec.description: Execute a statement on the currently open database.

dbexecstmt.description: Execute a statement with the currently open statement handle.

dbfetch.description: Fetch a row from the current database result.

dbfreestmt.description: Free the statement handle for the currently open database.

DICTphraselookup.description: Look up a phrase in the Knowledge Base dictionary by walking the parse tree from a starting node alongside a phrase hierarchy stored in the KB. Parse tree nodes that match the phrase hierarchy are annotated with match information.

excise.description: Excise the nodes matching the range of elements from ruleEltNumber1 to ruleEltNumber2 from the parse tree.

factorial.description: Compute the factorial of a non-negative integer.

fileout.description: Open fileName for appending. FileName becomes a variable useable in print actions with a file argument, e.g. prlit().

fprintgvar.description: OBSOLETE. To fileName, print the value of the global variable var.

fprintnvar.description: OBSOLETE. To fileName, print the value of the variable var in the ordth element's node.

fprintvar.description: Print the values of the global variable varName to fileName.

fprintxvar.description: OBSOLETE. To fileName, print the value of the variable var in the ordth context node.

gdump.description: Dump all the global variables and their values to fileName.

ginc.description: Increment the value of the global variable var. If the variable has no numeric value, it is set so that incrementing yields 1.

gtolower.description: Convert the strings in multi-string valued global variable to lower case.

guniq.description: Remove redundancies in a sorted, multi-string valued global variable.

inc.description: Increment the value of the global variable var. If the variable has no numeric value, it is set so that incrementing yields 1.

listadd.description: Add a new node to a list node's children. If the item occurs after the list (olist < oitem), it is added as the last child. If the item occurs before the list, it is added as the first child. The optional keep arg can be "true" or "false". If "true", it keeps the nodes between the list and the item as children of list. If "false", it excises all the intervening nodes.

loaddict.description: Load a single dictionary file (.dict) from the analyzer's kb/user directory into the conceptual grammar (the KB currently in memory).

loadkbb.description: Load a single knowledge base binary file (.kbb) from the analyzer's kb/user directory into the conceptual grammar (the KB currently in memory).

logten.description: Compute base 10 logarithm of given number.

lookup.description: Look up the values of named global variable var in the file named fileName and modify the named flag flag.

merge.description: Perform a single tier reduction that dissolves each top level node in the matched phrase.

merger.description: Perform a single tier reduction that dissolves each top level node in the matched range.

mod.description: Compute the modulus (remainder) of dividing one integer by another.

ndump.description: Dump all the variables in the ordth phrase element's node and their values to fileName.

ninc.description: Increment the variable var stored on the node matching the numth rule element. If the variable has no numeric value, it is set so that incrementing yields 1.

noop.description: Perform no POST Action. This is a convenience to disable the default single() reduce action.

or.description: Check action that succeeds if any of the rule elements from number1 to number2 has matched an actual node. Meaningful only if this is a range of optional rule elements.

pnmove.description: Programmatically move a node in the parse tree..

pnpush.description: Group a single parse tree node under a new nonliteral parent node.

pnpushval.description: Push a value onto the front of pnode's variable.

pnremoveval.description: Remove a variable from a parse tree node.

pnrpushval.description: Push a value onto the end of pnode's variable.

pnsetfired.description: Set the fired flag of a parse tree node.

pnvartype.description: Fetch the data type of a parse tree node's variable value.

pranchor.description: OBSOLETE. Print a web URL to fileName. Treat the range (number1, number2) as a URL.

prchild.description: OBSOLETE. Look for name immediately under the node matching rule element number. Print its text to fileName, if found.

print.description: OBSOLETE. Print the literal string to standard output.

printr.description: OBSOLETE. Print the text for the rule element range from number1 to number2 to standard output.

printvar.description: Print the values of the global variable varName to standard output.

prlit.description: OBSOLETE. Print the literal string string to fileName.

prrange.description: Print the text under a range of rule elements number1 to number2 to fileName.

prtree.description: Look for name anywhere under the node matching rule element number. Print its text to fileName, if found.

prxtree.description: OBSOLETE. To fileName, print the first node called name found in the ordth element's tree, preceded by prestr and followed by poststr. If named node is not found, nothing is printed out.

push.description: Push a value to the front of a variable's value(s).

randomint.description: Generate a random integer between two given integers, inclusive.

resolveurl.description: Convert a relative URL to an absolute URL.

sdump.description: Dump all the variables in the suggested node and their values to fileName.

setbase.description: Set the BASE attribute of the numth node to "true" or "false".

setlookahead.description: Dynamically set the next node that the rule matcher will look at, after the current rule match is done.

setunsealed.description: Set the UNSEALED attribute of the numth node to "true" or "false".

single.description: Perform the DEFAULT single tier reduction. This reduces the entire set of nodes that matched a rule phrase.

singler.description: Perform a single tier reduction of a range of rule elements. eg. If finding a period to be end-of-sentence in context, we only want to reduce the period to end-of-sentence, not the whole context.

singlex.description: Perform a single tier reduction of a range of rule elements. All nodes NOT in the range are EXCISED.

singlezap.description: Perform a single tier reduction. Excise all the nodes in the matched phrase.

sortvals.description: Sort the strings in multi-string-valued global variable varname.

splice.description: Dissolve the top level nodes of given range.

startout.description: Divert standard output to the main output file, set by the caller of the analyzer.

stopout.description: Stop diverting standard output to the main output file. Subsequent file-less output is to standard output.

strhaspunct.description: Check if a string contains a punctuation character.

striscaps.description: Check if the entire string is uppercase.

urlbase.description: Extract a normalized directory path from a URL.

urltofile.description: Fetch a page from the web using the given URL and writing to the given file name.

varstrs.description: Create an empty multi-string-valued global variable with name varname.

xaddlen.description: Add the text length of the node(s) matching the numth rule element to the variable var stored on the context (dominant) node.

xaddnvar.description: Add the value of the node variable nvar (taken from the node matching the numth rule element) to the variable xvar stored on the context (dominant) node.

xdump.description: Dump all the variables in the ordth context node and their values to fileName.

xinc.description: Increment the variable var stored on the context (dominant) node. If the variable has no numeric value, it is set so that incrementing yields 1.

xrename.description: Rename the numth context node to name. If num is absent or 0, rename last context node.

cap.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token has capitalized first letter.

length.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token has length exactly equal to lengthNumber.

lengthr.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token length is in the range of lengths (lengthNumber1,lengthNumber2), inclusive.

lowercase.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token is all lowercase.

numrange.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token is numeric in the given range.

regexp.description: Match text to a regular expression pattern.

regexpi.description: Match text to a regular expression pattern (case insensitive).

unknown.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token is an unknown word.

uppercase.description: Pre action that succeeds if leaf token is all uppercase.

var.description: Feature-based matching. Match if the given nodes have a variable called varName with non-zero value.

vareq.description: Feature-based matching. Match if the given nodes have a variable called varName whose value equals the string or number in the second argument.

vargt.description: Feature-based matching. Match if the given nodes have a variable called varName whose numeric value is greater than the number in the second argument.

varlt.description: Feature-based matching. Match if the given nodes have a variable called varName whose numeric value is less than the number in the second argument.

varne.description: Feature-based matching. Match if the given nodes have a variable called varName whose value doe not equal the string or number in the second argument.

varz.description: Feature-based matching. Match if the given nodes have a variable called varName with zero value.
